question.text: Rajesh needs Rs.30,000 per month today to meet his family expenses. If inflation is 6% per annum, what will be his approximate monthly expense requirement after 10 years?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Using the formula FV = PV × (1+r)^n: Rs.30,000 × (1.06)^10 = Rs.30,000 × 1.7908 = Rs.53,725 approximately. **The Trap:** Rs.48,000 comes from simply adding 6% of Rs.30,000 ten times (simple interest logic), which ignores compounding. Rs.60,000 is double, which would require roughly 12 years at 6%. **Cross-Exam Context:** Inflation-adjusted calculations appear in Series V-A (MF), X-A/X-B (Investment Adviser), and XV (Research Analyst) exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Use the Excel FV function or the Rule of 72 for quick estimates. At 6% inflation, costs double roughly every 12 years.
